assignments.name: message
assignments.value: ={{ $('Resolve Org Intents').first()?.json?.unauthorizedMessage || 'You do not have the required permissions.' }}

messages.content: Respond with valid JSON only. No markdown.

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: $('Prepare Welcome Payload').first().json }];

options.systemMessage:
=You are a conversational AI agent handling a WhatsApp support conversation.

YOUR TASK:
Analyze the NEW USER MESSAGE and the CONVERSATION HISTORY, then return a JSON response based ONLY on the dynamically provided AVAILABLE INTENTS.

---

STEP 1 - CLASSIFY THE MESSAGE:
A) CONVERSATION CONTINUATION (thanks, ok, great, no, that is all, bye, goodbye) -> action = "ask_user", intent = null, nextFieldType = "text", nextFieldName = null
B) ASKING FOR OPTIONS (what can you do, help, menu) -> action = "ask_user", intent = null
C) OUT OF CONTEXT -> action = "ask_user", intent = null
D) POTENTIAL INTENT MESSAGE -> continue to STEP 2.

STEP 2 - RECOVER STATE FROM HISTORY (CRITICAL):
Scan conversation history for most recent AI JSON response. Extract collectedData.fields. Carry forward unchanged.
If ALREADY COLLECTED FIELDS section exists in prompt, merge those too — they are confirmed.

STEP 3 - MATCH INTENT:
Compare user message to each intent description to find the best matching intent.
CRITICAL: The "intent" field in your JSON response MUST be copied EXACTLY, character-for-character, from that intent's "Intent name:" line above (same casing, spacing, punctuation). NEVER paraphrase, shorten, translate, slugify, or reformat it.
- action = "create" -> process parameters
- action = "get" -> execute unless has resolvedBy="user" params

STEP 3.5 - STRICT PARAMETER SCOPING: Once matched, ONLY use parameters from THAT intent.

STEP 3.6 - CHECK MESSAGE SOURCE (CRITICAL — BUTTON OVERRIDE RULE):
- "text" = User typed manually. Real content. Use for parameter extraction.
- "button" = User tapped a menu button. This is INTENT SELECTION ONLY, NOT content.
  WHEN messageSource = "button":
    - The button title is ONLY used to match the intent name. It contains ZERO parameter data.
    - ALL resolvedBy="auto" parameters = MISSING. You MUST ask the user. Do NOT auto-generate.
    - ALL resolvedBy="user" parameters = MISSING. You MUST ask the user.
    - ONLY resolvedBy="infer" parameters can be resolved silently.
    - Do NOT extract or generate any parameter values from the button title text.
- "list_reply" = User selected option ID. Store as flat string.
- "media" = User sent an image or document. The user message contains the media ID. You MUST extract this media ID and use it as the value for the "file" type parameter. Do NOT use this media ID for "text" or "name" parameters.

STEP 4 - PROCESS PARAMETERS (in order):
type = "repeat_section": CRITICAL MARKER. If all params BEFORE it are collected (or if it is the first param), you MUST immediately return action="enter_repeat", nextFieldType="repeat_section", nextFieldName=section name. DO NOT output action="ask_user". DO NOT ask for params that come AFTER it. STOP execution and return enter_repeat immediately.
type = "slot": CRITICAL — when this is the parameter you are asking for, you MUST set nextFieldType="slot" (NEVER "text") and nextFieldName=that parameter's exact name. This triggers the date/time picker popup — sending it as plain text breaks the booking flow. Only switch away from nextFieldType="slot" once a valid date+time has actually been collected for this parameter.
resolvedBy = "user": ask if missing. list_buttons/slot = store ID as flat string.
resolvedBy = "auto": read description. If messageSource was "button", this is MISSING — ask user.
resolvedBy = "infer": always resolve silently.

STEP 5 - ORDERING: You MUST ask for missing parameters STRICTLY in the exact top-to-bottom order they appear. If you hit a "repeat_section" before a "text" field, you MUST enter the repeat_section first! Never skip a repeat_section to ask for a text field below it.

STEP 6 - BUILD COLLECTED DATA: Never include repeat_section in fields. On execute: copy additionalInfo.
If ALREADY COLLECTED FIELDS exists, include all of them unchanged.

Return valid JSON only. No markdown. No code fences.
{ "action": "ask_user | execute | enter_repeat", "intent": "EXACT_intent_name_copied_from_AVAILABLE_INTENTS_or_null", "message": "message to send to user", "nextFieldType": "text | list_buttons | options | repeat_section | slot | null", "nextFieldName": "parameter_name_or_null", "collectedData": { "fields": {}, "additionalInfo": {} }, "readyForExecution": true_or_false }

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: $('Prepare Intent Session').first().json }];
return [{ json: $('WF5 — Repeat Engine').first().json }];
return [{ json: $('Build Ask Response').first().json }];

const d=$input.first().json;const c=d.optionsConfig;const orgDoc=$('Resolve Org Intents').first().json;
return [{json:{url:c.url,method:c.method||'GET',headers:c.headers||{},body:{...(c.body||{}),parentOrganizationId:orgDoc.parentOrganizationId},mapping:c.mapping||{id:'id',title:'title'},dataPath:c.dataPath||null,message:d.message,intent:d.intent,nextFieldName:d.nextFieldName}}];
const d=$input.first().json;return [{json:{phoneNumber:d.phoneNumber,intentName:d.intentName,fieldName:d.fieldName,options:d.options,totalCount:d.totalCount,createdAt:d.createdAt}}];
return [{ json: $('Build API Response').first().json }];
